name: sf-recall
description: Binary-assertion test suite for /ren:recall. Validates wiki-grep ranking, read-only invariant, and empty-query refusal.

tests.expected_output_summary: Wiki match for the postgres-vs-mongo decision page ranks first; snippet includes the matching line.
binary_assertions: The user-facing output contains the path 'wiki/decisions/' followed by a slug containing 'postgres'
The first listed wiki hit has higher score than any subsequent hit
Each listed wiki hit's snippet contains at least one query token (case-insensitive)
No file under wiki/ was modified by the run (sha256 checksums of all wiki/**/*.md unchanged)

tests.expected_output_summary: Query tokens don't appear in any wiki page. Skill prints an explicit no-results message.
binary_assertions: User-facing output contains the substring 'No matches' (or equivalent explicit no-results phrasing)
No error or stack trace appears in the output

tests.expected_output_summary: Skill refuses with a usage prompt.
binary_assertions: User-facing output contains the substring 'Empty query' OR 'Usage: /ren:recall'
No file under wiki/ is read for content matching (skill exits before grep)

tests.expected_output_summary: After the run, every wiki file's content is byte-identical to its pre-run state (the LOAD-BEARING invariant).
binary_assertions: sha256 of every file under wiki/**/*.md is unchanged after the run
No new files are created under wiki/ during the run
No files are deleted from wiki/ during the run
Git status (if wiki is a git repo) shows zero modified/untracked/deleted files post-run
